articles.description:
Denny's 2025 holiday menu has arrived, anchored by the returning Rudolph Pancakes, which are themed after Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer.

The pancake is available on its own and as part of an Everyday Value Slam.
An à la carte order of Rudolph Pancakes features two buttermilk pancakes arranged to make Rudolph's head decorated with turkey bacon antlers, whipped cream dollops, chocolate eyes, and a strawberry for Rudolph's red nose.
The Rudolph Everyday Value Slam includes Rudolph Pancakes and two eggs. Prices vary, but the Slam is priced at $6.99 near me in the Los Angeles area, while the pancakes alone are $5.
As a holiday promotion, Denny's Kids Eat Free deal is now available every day, from 4PM to 10PM, for a limited time. Under the terms of the offer, you can get a free Kids Meal with the purchase of each adult entree when dining in at participating locations nationwide.
Other seasonal holiday items available now at Denny's include:
- New Cinnamon Apple Crisp Pancake Slam - Two buttermilk pancakes cooked with cinnamon crumb topping, topped with warm apple crisp, salted caramel sauce, and powdered sugar. The pancakes are served with two eggs, hash browns, and two applewood-smoked bacon strips or two pork sausage links.
- New Cold Brew Flavors - For a limited time, you can get cold brew coffee in three new seasonal flavors: 


Salted Caramel - Cold brew mixed with milk and caramel syrup, poured over ice, and topped with whipped cream and caramel sauce.
Peppermint Mocha - Cold brew mixed with milk and peppermint syrup, poured over ice, and topped with whipped cream and chocolate sauce.
Sweet & Creamy - Cold brew mixed with milk and cane syrup, poured over ice.


- New Peppermint Hot Chocolate - Hot chocolate mixed with peppermint syrup and topped with whipped cream.
Photo via Denny's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Denny's 2025 Holiday Menu Arrives

articles.description:
Dutch Bros readies for the holidays with the release of its 2025 holiday menu, featuring new and returning seasonal drinks.

This year's holiday menu is available now at all Dutch Bros locations for a limited time and includes:
- New Mistletoe Rebel - Raspberry and pomegranate flavors mixed with the chain's signature Rebel energy drink, and decorated with a green glitter glaze. You can get it blended, iced, or as a lemonade (a non-caffeinated option).
- New Holiday Cookie Freeze - Espresso, sweet cream, and vanilla, swirled with jingle sprinkles and finished with Soft Top and more jingle sprinkles. You can also get it as a shake or iced latte.
- New Jingle Nog Latte - Espresso and holiday nog, topped with Soft Top and jingle sprinkles.
- New Jingle Chai Nog - Chai and holiday nog, topped with Soft Top and jingle sprinkles.
- Hazelnut Truffle Mocha - A hazelnut mocha topped with Soft Top and a caramel drizzle. You can get it iced, hot, or as a Freeze. 
-  Candy Cane Mocha - Espresso, peppermint flavor, and chocolate milk, crowned with Soft Top and candy cane sprinkles. You can get it iced, hot, as a Freeze, as a cold brew, or as a hot cocoa. 
This year's holiday drinks come in one of six holiday-themed cups. Iced holiday drinks also come with a limited-edition straw.
Photo via Dutch Bros.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Dutch Bros Welcomes 2025 Holiday Menu

articles.description:
Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion features a Vanilla (or Chocolate) Frosty swirled with new snickerdoodle sauce and topped with snickerdoodle cookie crumbles.

It cost me $4.19 for a small, and I went with the vanilla version.



Unlike a McFlurry or a Blizzard, the Frosty Fusion is only mixed for a few seconds (if that). The sauce remained mostly drizzled on the sides and bottom of the cup, resulting in a mild cinnamon note to the creamy Vanilla Frosty and a much sweeter, more pronounced cinnamon taste whenever the sauce was encountered. Interestingly, the sauce contained visible flecks of cinnamon.
The snickerdoodle cookie crumbles on top had the requisite mix of sweet vanilla and cinnamon flavors. They were super crunchy (although this experience may vary), providing a nice contrast to the smooth Frosty and liquid sauce.



Overall, I enjoyed Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion a good deal. I like snickerdoodles, and the flavor was captured well here. I especially liked the hearty crunch of the cookies on top as that can be hit-or-miss with this type of dessert.
Nutritional Info - Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion
Calories - 410
Fat - 11g (Saturated Fat - 6g)
Sodium - 320mg
Carbs - 70g (Sugar - 56g)
Protein - 9g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Wendy's - Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion
